title: About
excerpt: 25 years of web, distilled into something better.
meta_title: About Idun Works — The team behind the CMS
meta_description: A small studio with big ideas. Meet the team behind the Idun CMS — 25 years of web craft meets AI precision.

settings.title: Built by people who got tired of the alternatives.
settings.subtitle: Three rebels who spent 25 years building on other platforms. Then we built our own.

settings.content:
The Short Version

Idun Works is a small web studio based in Sweden. We build fast, beautiful websites using our own CMS — Idun.

We’re not an agency with 50 people and a ping-pong table. We’re three: Tibbe, a web developer with 25 years in the game, Brett, a mean fullstack dev all the way from Järpen, Jämtland, and Buttler, an AI that never sleeps and never ships half-baked code.

Today's Feature: Brett Simpson

Speaking of unlikely meetings... Tibbe and Brett first encountered each other while hanggliding, literally shouting to each other mid-air. From there, they became partners for many years, surviving rough times together in the WordPress trenches.

Brett's originally from Canada but landed in Järpen, Jämtland — which makes him practically a neighbor in Swedish terms. Like Tibbe, he's a WordPress veteran who's ready to move on and stay true to our beliefs about what web development should be.

The Long Version

Tibbe started building websites in the early 2000s. He contributed to WordPress core. Brett joined the journey along the way, bringing his fullstack expertise to every project. Together, they’ve installed, configured, and cursed at roughly 30 different content management systems. Drupal, Joomla, Ghost, Strapi, Sanity, Contentful, Payload — you name it.

They all share the same arc: start with big promises, end with lock-in, bloat, and a support ticket that goes nowhere.

So in 2024, Tibbe started building Idun with Brett’s partnership — a CMS that does what site owners actually need:

  • Pages and blog posts with a visual editor
  • Contact forms that just work
  • A shop if you need one
  • Online courses if you teach
  • Email campaigns built in
  • Analytics that respect privacy
  • Multi-language support
  • An AI assistant that helps you write

All of it toggleable. All of it fast. All of it yours.

Why “Idun”?

In Norse mythology, Idun is the goddess who tends the apples of youth — she keeps things alive and fresh. That’s what a good CMS should do: keep your site alive without you having to fight it every day.

Also, a friend’s daughter is named Idun, and she sometimes helps out. So it felt right.

settings.title: Want to work with us?
settings.description: We take on a handful of projects at a time. If you need a fast, honest website — let’s talk.
